metadata.title: What to Do After a Car Accident in Wichita
metadata.description: Learn the essential steps to take after a car accident in Wichita, from medical care and evidence collection to insurance and legal guidance.

what to do after a wreck

hero.label: POST-CRASH ACTION PLAN
hero.title: What to Do After a Car Accident in Wichita

author.name: Bradley A. Pistotnik (Trial Lawyer)
author.bio: Brad Pistotnik is the CEO of Bull Attorneys®, P.A. and a Kansas trucking and personal injury lawyer with over 45 years of experience. He focuses on serious injury and truck accident cases involving DOT regulations and commercial vehicle safety standards.

sections.content: A car accident can leave you shaken up fast. Even a crash that looks small at first can lead to pain, missed work, repair bills, and stress with the insurance company. Know the steps that protect your health, your claim, and your peace of mind.

items.heading: Get to Safety and Call 911
items.text: If you can move safely, get out of traffic and call 911. If anyone is hurt, ask for medical help right away.

items.heading: Get Medical Care
items.text: Not every injury shows up right away. Pain, headache, dizziness, numbness, or trouble moving — get checked by a doctor. Early treatment helps recovery and creates a record.

items.heading: Take Photos and Gather Information
items.text: Photograph both vehicles, damage, the road, traffic signs, skid marks, debris, and visible injuries. Get driver names, phone numbers, insurance info, plates, and witness contacts.

items.heading: Report the Crash Carefully
items.text: Stick to the facts. Don't guess about fault. Don't say you're fine if you're still in pain. Keep notes about every call, email, and message.

items.heading: Watch the First Few Days Closely
items.text: Pain may get worse. Track pain levels, doctor visits, medicines, time missed from work, sleep, and daily activities.

items.heading: Know That Accident Risks Are Still Serious
items.text: U.S. traffic deaths fell 6.7% from 2024 to 2025 (NHTSA), but crashes still cause serious injuries and deaths every year.

items.heading: Be Extra Careful if a Truck or Company Vehicle Was Involved
items.text: These claims may involve company records, higher insurance limits, and extra safety rules — and may require faster investigation.

items.heading: Get Help If the Accident Was Serious
items.text: Major injuries, missed work, disputed fault, insurance pressure, a commercial vehicle, or a fatal crash — the more serious the case, the more important early legal answers become.

sections.label: U.S. TRAFFIC DEATHS: 2024 VS 2025
sections.heading: Traffic Crashes Remain a Major Public Safety Problem

sections.label: DOCUMENT YOUR RECOVERY

image.alt: Damaged car at a crash scene in Wichita

sections.heading: Keep a Simple Notebook for the First 30 Days
sections.intro: A few minutes of notes each day can powerfully show how the crash affected your life.

faqs.question: What should I do first after a car accident in Wichita?
faqs.answer: Get to safety if you can, call 911, and get medical help if anyone is hurt or if you may be injured.

faqs.question: Should I see a doctor even if I feel okay after a wreck?
faqs.answer: Yes. Some injuries do not show up right away. Pain, dizziness, headaches, and other symptoms may appear hours later or the next day.

faqs.question: What information should I collect after a car accident?
faqs.answer: Take photos of the vehicles, road, traffic signs, skid marks, and visible injuries. Also collect driver information, insurance details, and witness contact information.

faqs.question: What should I tell the insurance company after an accident?
faqs.answer: Stick to the facts, do not guess about fault, and do not downplay your injuries if you are still in pain or waiting on medical care.

faqs.question: When should I get legal help after a car accident?
faqs.answer: You may want legal help if you have major injuries, missed work, a disputed claim, insurance pressure, a truck or company vehicle involved, or a fatal crash.

schema.articleBody: After a car accident in Wichita, the first step is to get to safety and call 911. Get medical care even if you feel fine — many injuries appear hours or days later. Photograph vehicles, damage, skid marks, and the scene. Collect driver information, insurance details, and witness contacts. Report the crash carefully and stick to the facts. Track your symptoms, doctor visits, and missed work in the first 30 days. Be extra cautious if a truck or commercial vehicle was involved. U.S. traffic deaths fell from 39,254 in 2024 to 36,640 in 2025, but crashes remain a serious public safety concern. If your injuries are serious or the insurance company is pressuring you, contact a personal injury attorney.

disclaimer: This blog provides general information only, not legal advice. Reading it does not create an attorney-client relationship. Every case is different. Results vary depending on each case's facts and legal circumstances. Deadlines and laws can change, so speak directly with an attorney about your accident.
